sources.read: Reference Execution Description canonicalComparisonPathFamily order used by semantic trials.
Measured setpiece contract order generated from challenge motion/spec evidence.
Current target-contract artifact consumed by strict challenge-stage conformance.
Current hard gate for challenge motion/profile path-order drift.
Stage 7 layout groupPathFamilies in the Aurora runtime source.
Stage 7 motionSpecGroups pathFamilyHint values consumed first by spawnChallenge.
Stage 7 runtime contract group pathFamily declarations exposed to harnesses.

truthAlignment.candidateGatePolicy: A semantic candidate is source-ready only when its projected full path-family order matches all live promotion gates and its movement transforms map to consumed runtime fields.
truthAlignment.canonicalForRuntimeCandidateGates: Current live runtime-candidate gates are the restored runtime source, challenge-stage target-contract artifact, and check-challenge-motion-profile expected order. The Reference Execution Description and challenge-setpiece contract are measured reference intent, but they are not sufficient to pass live promotion until the gate authority is explicitly reconciled.

mismatches.read: reference-execution-description path-family order differs from the current live promotion gate order.
challenge-setpiece-contracts path-family order differs from the current live promotion gate order.

truthAlignment.staleOrDiagnosticRead: Measured reference intent disagrees with the restored live gate order. Treat this as an unresolved source-of-truth conflict; do not update old gates merely to pass the rejected candidate.

sourceReadyGate.read: Source-ready now means a candidate can prove both semantic score intent and concrete runtime/source expressibility against live promotion gates, while preserving target-conformance debt when RED/setpiece intent disagrees.

candidates.intendedPlayerFacingMeaning: Combine the strongest measured phase-window signal with live-authority path families and explicit late-group protection.

warnings: group 2 uses live promotion family cross-sweep while RED target family is hook-arc
group 4 uses live promotion family hook-arc while RED target family is cross-sweep
group 5 uses live promotion family boss-led-loop while RED target family is hook-arc
phase-duration rebalance needs browser capture before promotion because timing changes can affect spacing.
semanticValidity.read: Semantic validity passed; numeric movement remains subject to the trial promotion gate.

generatedRuntimeControls.expectedVisibleEffect: moves the group visible window earlier or later relative to other groups
changes when the reference path enters its exit/overrun behavior
stretches or compresses reference path playback in real browser-visible time
none until the runtime consumes exitS

classMappings.runtimeConsumer: spawnChallenge() consumes groupSpawnOffsets or motionSpecGroup.spawnOffsetS; applyReferenceChallengePath() consumes phaseDurations.trackS; the challenge update loop consumes referencePath.playbackScale. phaseDurations.exitS is declared but not currently consumed.
classMappings.predictionLimits: The trial score can improve by assigning visible windows directly. Source readiness requires compiledRuntimeControls plus proof that the emitted controls produce browser-visible timing/path movement.
classMappings.remainingCompilerGap: Generated candidates now emit compiledRuntimeControls and cite the proof artifact. Source readiness remains blocked until the compiled proof passes the motion/profile proxy and preserves protected group 4/group 5 timing; visibleStartS/visibleEndS alone remains analysis-only.

classMappings.runtimeConsumer: No runtime source field changes; this class carries shot-opportunity evidence as an invariant.
classMappings.predictionLimits: Cannot make a candidate source-ready by itself.

classMappings.runtimeConsumer: No runtime source field changes unless another transform touches groups 4 or 5; this class preserves measured group floors.
classMappings.predictionLimits: A predictedRuntimeVector used only for protection is not proof that a source edit can express a new vector.

runtimeExpressibility.read: Runtime expressibility failed; the candidate may remain useful analysis but is not ready for source promotion.

analysisCompilerMappings.semanticMeaning: Reduce lower-field overstay by translating target lower-field share into runtime lower-field bias controls.
analysisCompilerMappings.expectedVisibleEffect: Moves the affected group vertically and changes late lower-field dwell, but the lowerFieldShare transfer function is not proven for Stage 7.
analysisCompilerMappings.proofHarnessRequired: future Stage 7 lower-field expressibility proof plus challenge-motion-profile and strict challenge-stage conformance

analysisCompilerMappings.semanticMeaning: Reduce lower-field overstay by translating target lower-field share into runtime lower-field bias controls.
analysisCompilerMappings.expectedVisibleEffect: Moves the affected group vertically and changes late lower-field dwell, but the lowerFieldShare transfer function is not proven for Stage 7.
analysisCompilerMappings.proofHarnessRequired: future Stage 7 lower-field expressibility proof plus challenge-motion-profile and strict challenge-stage conformance

classMappings.runtimeConsumer: Stage 7 motion-spec controls are consumed by the challenge path generator, but lowerFieldShare is not itself a runtime field.
classMappings.predictionLimits: The compiler can derive lowerFieldBias/yOffset from target lower-field share using the project trajectory-control formula, but no Stage 7 proof shows the emitted values produce the intended lower-field-share change without readability regression.
classMappings.remainingCompilerGap: Verify concrete lowerFieldBias/yOffset controls against actual browser lower-field share, spacing/readability, and scoreable-route guardrails.

generatedRuntimeControls.expectedVisibleEffect: speeds or slows reference path playback; may change sampled visible path length, but the Stage 7 path-length transfer is not proof-backed

analysisCompilerMappings.semanticMeaning: Compress group 1 aggregate path length toward the RED target while preserving the live cross-sweep family.
analysisCompilerMappings.expectedVisibleEffect: Speeds reference-path playback and may shorten sampled visible-route length, but the path-length transfer function is not proven for Stage 7.
analysisCompilerMappings.proofHarnessRequired: future Stage 7 path-length expressibility proof plus challenge-motion-profile and strict challenge-stage conformance

analysisCompilerMappings.semanticMeaning: Compress group 1 aggregate path length toward the RED target while preserving the live cross-sweep family.
analysisCompilerMappings.expectedVisibleEffect: Speeds reference-path playback and may shorten sampled visible-route length, but the path-length transfer function is not proven for Stage 7.
analysisCompilerMappings.proofHarnessRequired: future Stage 7 path-length expressibility proof plus challenge-motion-profile and strict challenge-stage conformance

classMappings.runtimeConsumer: The challenge update loop consumes referencePath.playbackScale, but no current runtime source field consumes abstract pathLength directly.
classMappings.predictionLimits: The trial vector can shorten pathLength mathematically, and the compiler can derive a playbackScale candidate from baseline path length over target path length, but no browser proof shows that this improves actual Stage 7 object-track path length safely.
classMappings.remainingCompilerGap: Prove the playbackScale/path-geometry transfer against browser object tracks before allowing source promotion.

analysisCompilerMappings.semanticMeaning: Compress group 1 aggregate path length toward the RED target while preserving the live cross-sweep family.
analysisCompilerMappings.expectedVisibleEffect: Speeds reference-path playback and may shorten sampled visible-route length, but the path-length transfer function is not proven for Stage 7.
analysisCompilerMappings.proofHarnessRequired: future Stage 7 path-length expressibility proof plus challenge-motion-profile and strict challenge-stage conformance

analysisCompilerMappings.semanticMeaning: Compress group 1 aggregate path length toward the RED target while preserving the live cross-sweep family.

classMappings.runtimeConsumer: The challenge update loop consumes referencePath.playbackScale, but no current runtime source field consumes abstract pathLength directly.
classMappings.predictionLimits: The trial vector can shorten pathLength mathematically, and the compiler can derive a playbackScale candidate from baseline path length over target path length, but no browser proof shows that this improves actual Stage 7 object-track path length safely.
classMappings.remainingCompilerGap: Prove the playbackScale/path-geometry transfer against browser object tracks before allowing source promotion.

generatedRuntimeControls.expectedVisibleEffect: adds lower-field vertical bias after the reference-path phase passes its lower-field gate
moves the group's generated path envelope vertically

analysisCompilerMappings.semanticMeaning: Reduce lower-field overstay by translating target lower-field share into runtime lower-field bias controls.
analysisCompilerMappings.expectedVisibleEffect: Moves the affected group vertically and changes late lower-field dwell, but the lowerFieldShare transfer function is not proven for Stage 7.
analysisCompilerMappings.proofHarnessRequired: future Stage 7 lower-field expressibility proof plus challenge-motion-profile and strict challenge-stage conformance

analysisCompilerMappings.semanticMeaning: Reduce lower-field overstay by translating target lower-field share into runtime lower-field bias controls.
analysisCompilerMappings.expectedVisibleEffect: Moves the affected group vertically and changes late lower-field dwell, but the lowerFieldShare transfer function is not proven for Stage 7.
analysisCompilerMappings.proofHarnessRequired: future Stage 7 lower-field expressibility proof plus challenge-motion-profile and strict challenge-stage conformance

classMappings.runtimeConsumer: Stage 7 motion-spec controls are consumed by the challenge path generator, but lowerFieldShare is not itself a runtime field.
classMappings.predictionLimits: The compiler can derive lowerFieldBias/yOffset from target lower-field share using the project trajectory-control formula, but no Stage 7 proof shows the emitted values produce the intended lower-field-share change without readability regression.
classMappings.remainingCompilerGap: Verify concrete lowerFieldBias/yOffset controls against actual browser lower-field share, spacing/readability, and scoreable-route guardrails.

classMappings.runtimeConsumer: spawnChallenge() consumes groupSpawnOffsets or motionSpecGroup.spawnOffsetS; applyReferenceChallengePath() consumes phaseDurations.trackS; the challenge update loop consumes referencePath.playbackScale. phaseDurations.exitS is declared but not currently consumed.
classMappings.predictionLimits: The trial score can improve by assigning visible windows directly. Source readiness requires compiledRuntimeControls plus proof that the emitted controls produce browser-visible timing/path movement.
classMappings.remainingCompilerGap: Generated candidates now emit compiledRuntimeControls and cite the proof artifact. Source readiness remains blocked until the compiled proof passes the motion/profile proxy and preserves protected group 4/group 5 timing; visibleStartS/visibleEndS alone remains analysis-only.

proofBackedImprovement.read: Runtime controls showed browser-visible movement, but no movement class has a passing proof-backed source-readiness claim.

summary.read: No semantic candidate passed the full pre-source promotion gate; preserve this batch as measurement evidence and refine the failing transformation classes.

nextBestStep: Refine the semantic compiler before touching runtime source: keep Stage 7 source-ready path families aligned with live gates, keep phase-duration motion/profile-compatible while preserving group 4/group 5 timing, and add proof harnesses for path-length/lower-field mappings.
